objective: Implement two disabled-by-default offline deep Modules that own symmetric order-regime diagnosis and pure evidence projection without creating any launch or persistence surface.

allowed_file_scope.rule: All immutable records, Protocols, errors, and helper functions required by this milestone live in the two new files. Import them by direct module path; do not export them from eoh_rag.fme.__init__ yet.

interface.construction: OrderRegimeDiagnosticCoordinator(evaluator: OrderRegimeEvaluator, cache: MutableMapping[str, OrderRegimeCandidateDiagnostic] | None = None)
interface.operation: diagnose(checkpoint: FrozenCandidateCheckpoint, policy: OrderRegimeDiagnosticPolicy = OrderRegimeDiagnosticPolicy()) -> OrderRegimeDiagnosticOutcome
interface.side_effects: No file, network, provider, LLM, manifest, controller, archive, or RAG side effects. The only permitted mutation is writing exact-key candidate diagnostics to the injected in-memory cache when policy.enabled is true.

OrderRegimeDiagnosticPolicy.frozen_v1_values: All fields except enabled are validated against these exact v1 values; callers cannot use this record to tune the frozen experiment.

adapter_protocol.name: OrderRegimeEvaluator
adapter_protocol.production_adapter_future: A separately authorized BP evaluator bound to a frozen code source; not implemented in this milestone.
adapter_protocol.verification_adapter_future: A deterministic in-memory Adapter; tests are not created or run in this milestone.

input_invariants: Policy disabled is valid and returns before reading checkpoint candidates, cache, or evaluator.
Enabled policy values exactly match the frozen v1 constants; configuration drift is rejected.
observed_scope equals dev_only and trigger_reason is one of aggregate_near_tie, suspected_order_fragility, or pending_counterexample_comparison.
Candidate count is 2-4; candidate_id and code_sha256 are identical valid lowercase SHA-256 values and unique within the checkpoint.
Every candidate is feasible, has no failure_type, has a finite primary_objective, and has a non-empty primary_evaluation_hash.
aggregate_near_tie requires at least one non-best candidate within absolute objective distance 0.01 of the best.
profile, evaluator, and feedback-module hashes are valid lowercase SHA-256 values.
reserved_item_placements is at least candidate_count * 12 * 2048 before any evaluator call.
determinism: Sort candidates by candidate_id before cache lookup and evaluation.
candidate_set_sha256 hashes the ordered candidate IDs, primary evaluation hashes, trigger reason, and implementation hashes.
The exact candidate cache key hashes candidate code, profile, evaluator, and feedback-module hashes only.
Instantiate one OrderRegimeRankingTracker per diagnose call and record complete summaries in candidate_id order.
Canonical JSON SHA-256 excludes runtime_seconds and cache hit/miss counters from scientific identity but includes coordinate status and failure hashes.
cache_semantics: Disabled and rejected/skipped checkpoints never read or write cache entries.
Both complete and failed exact-key diagnostics are reusable, preventing silent retries of failures.
A cache entry whose candidate_id or cache_key disagrees with the requested coordinate is a contract error.
The coordinator never persists the cache and never accepts path configuration.
budget_semantics: Reserve the entire symmetric plan before the first cache lookup or evaluator call.
Cache hits do not alter the frozen planned or reserved cost; cache_hits and cache_misses separately describe actual evaluator demand.
Evaluate every cache miss exactly once even if another candidate fails, preserving all coordinates.
No retry, replacement candidate, partial success promotion, or threshold adjustment is permitted.

error_modes.behavior: Return status=skipped, reason=disabled, zero planned cost, and do not touch dependencies.
Return status=skipped, reason=insufficient_reserved_budget, and do not touch cache or evaluator.

error_modes.condition: checkpoint or policy violates a scientific invariant
error_modes.behavior: Raise OrderRegimeContractError with a stable error_code and no raw payload in the message.

error_modes.condition: evaluator raises or returns a malformed diagnostic
error_modes.behavior: Convert that coordinate to status=failed with a class-name failure_type and hashed sanitized message, continue remaining coordinates, and return checkpoint status=inconclusive.

error_modes.condition: any coordinate is failed or a complete summary has wrong candidate or visible scope
error_modes.behavior: Return status=inconclusive; do not emit ranking flips from failed coordinates.

interface.side_effects: Pure in-process transformation; no cache, file, archive, controller, prompt, RAG, provider, or network mutation.

BoundedPromptSummary.limits: At most two lexically sorted counterexample IDs; summary_text at most 600 characters and generated from fixed templates only.

input_invariants: Accept only complete or inconclusive outcomes with visible_scope=dev_only and a valid outcome_sha256.
A skipped outcome projects to empty tuples and pending_counterexample_comparisons=0 without inventing evidence.
Complete candidate diagnostics must contain summaries whose candidate IDs and behavior hashes match the outcome.
Failed coordinates may be represented in decision input hashes but never produce archive candidates, scoped memories, or prompt summaries.
projection_semantics: pending_counterexample_comparisons equals the number of distinct ranking-flip pair keys, not the number of candidate pairs.
Archive candidates are metadata-only records derived from distinguishing counterexamples; no call to FMEArchives occurs.
source_actor is copied from the frozen candidate and is never rewritten as research_agent.
Memory deduplication identity hashes behavior_profile_hash, applicability_scope, and outcome_sha256.
Prompt summaries use fixed templates and include no candidate code, raw items, paths, provider fields, held-out values, or free-form model text.
All tuples are lexically ordered and all hashes use canonical JSON.
error_modes: Raise OrderRegimeProjectionError with a stable error_code for outcome hash mismatch, scope mismatch, candidate mismatch, malformed summary, or forbidden raw-looking field.
Never partially project a malformed complete diagnostic.
An inconclusive outcome may project valid completed coordinates plus explicit decision hashes for failures, but pending comparisons are derived only from verified flips.

static_acceptance: Only the two allowed new files appear in the code diff.
Both files parse with Python AST and contain no CLI parser, main block, subprocess, socket, HTTP, provider, environment-variable, pathlib write, open, or persistence call.
The default policy literal is enabled=False and diagnose returns before evaluator/cache access when disabled.
No import from batch_runner, eoh_single_runner, recorder, rag_context_builder, provider, or official_eoh is added.
The implementation imports only existing pure records/Modules needed for summaries and canonical hashing, or implements local canonical hashing without side effects.
A static secret-shape scan, absolute-path scan, forbidden-import scan, FME v2 anchor hash check, git diff --check, and changed-file inventory pass.
No tests, type checker, full build, candidate evaluation, profile construction, manifest validation, API/LLM call, or cohort launch occurs in the implementation tick.
suggested_future_tests_not_authorized_by: Disabled policy causes zero evaluator and cache calls.
Insufficient budget skips before dependency access.
Two-candidate complete checkpoint produces deterministic outcome and projection hashes.
One evaluator failure still attempts every remaining coordinate and returns inconclusive.
Failed cache entries are reused without retry.
Malformed candidate/cache/summary identity raises the stable contract error.
Projection never includes raw code, items, held-out values, paths, or provider data.
exit_conditions: Stop before implementation if either new Module cannot be implemented without touching an existing file.
Stop if a runner, manifest, recorder, controller, archive, generic RAG, official_eoh, or FME v2 change appears necessary.
Stop if disabled behavior cannot be proven dependency-free by static inspection.
Stop if evaluator failure cannot be represented without dropping or retrying coordinates.
Stop if memory scope and evidence level cannot be enforced in typed records before CorpusItem conversion.
Stop if FME v2 manifest SHA-256 differs from 3a7b8127c18b32aac2728ba8e80479209517336cd8a3e612d76178becb8346e5.

evidence_refs.purpose: static seam audit and P0 gaps
existing pure compiler and ranking tracker
